code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in the",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is the leader of",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "is the language of",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Helper to clean quoted objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Build a single fluent sentence by iterating over triples in order
    sentence_parts = []
    prev_subject = None

    for tr in triples:
        subj = tr.subject
        pred = tr.predicate
        obj = clean_obj(tr.object)

        # Determine phrasing
        if pred in {"leader", "capital", "language"}:
            # Inverted phrasing to match expected style
            if pred == "leader":
                clause = f"{obj} is the leader of {subj}"
            elif pred == "capital":
                clause = f"The capital of {subj} is {obj}"
            else:  # language
                clause = f"{obj} is the language of {subj}"
        elif pred == "inaugurationDate":
            clause = f"The inauguration of {subj} was on {obj}"
        elif pred == "buildingStartDate":
            clause = f"{subj}'s building start was in {obj}"
        else:
            verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
            # Special handling for counts to add units
            if pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} floors"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"

        # Attach clause to the growing sentence
        if prev_subject is None:
            # First clause, start with subject if not already included
            if pred in {"leader", "capital", "language", "inaugurationDate", "buildingStartDate"}:
                sentence_parts.append(clause)
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(f"{subj} {clause}")
        elif subj == prev_subject:
            # Same subject: add as a relative clause
            sentence_parts.append(f", {clause}")
        else:
            # New subject: end previous sentence and start a new one
            sentence_parts.append(f". {subj} {clause}")

        prev_subject = subj

    # Join parts and ensure proper punctuation
    text = "".join(sentence_parts)
    if not text.endswith("."):
        text += "."
    # Capitalize first

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
